---
title: "Digital literacy: Data preparation"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true
    toc: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: true
  message: false
---

**Task**: Deidentifies and saves data for replication

# Setup

```{r}
#| label: data-setup-packages

# Load packages
pacman::p_load(tidyverse, haven, knitr)
```

# Paths and labels

```{r}
#| label: data-paths-and-labels

# This file requires access to full data
data_path <- "../../1_Projects/Covid Vaccine Allocation/4_data/"

# Large video files
df_09v <- read_dta(
  paste0(
    data_path,
    "wave_9/Full-Launch/paper_tiktok/3_merged_data/w9_video_reshaped.dta"))

df_10v <- read_dta(
  paste0(data_path, "wave_10/merged/Video_reshaped.dta"))

# Wave 9 and 10 combined data
df_w10 <- 
  read_dta("../../1_Projects/Covid Vaccine Allocation/4_data/wave_10/merged/144461 HU Berlin Impftracker Welle 10 - FINAL_Destring_-_Merged_Respondents_Enriched.dta") 


# Edit figure labels here
strategies <- 
  c("political\n(emotional)", "political\n(neutral)")

ps <- tibble(
  parties = c("AfD", "BSW", "CDU/CSU", "FDP", "Grüne", "Linke", "SPD"),
  party_stub = c("afd", "bsw", "cdu", "fdp", "gruene", "linke", "spd")
)
```

# Helper functions


```{r}
#| label: data-helper-functions

# Video data preparation
prepare_data <- function(df) {
  df <- df |>
    # Keeping ID which is used for linking periods but no PII
    # Dropping long text fields
    select(-any_of(c("USER", "record", 
                     "videoCurrentTimep1r", "video_desc"))) |>
    filter(SwitchedToRandom == 1) |>
    filter(!duration_video_ %in% c(-9, NA)) |>
    mutate(
      workshop_Treatment = ifelse(workshop_Treatment == 2, 0, as.numeric(workshop_Treatment)),
      strategy_2 = case_when(
        content == "entertainment" ~ "entertainment",
        strategy == "neutral" ~ strategies[2],
        strategy %in% c("anger", "fear", "superiority") ~ strategies[1]
      ),
      party = party |> str_replace("_", "/"),
      partisan = NA
    ) |>
    group_by(ID) |>
    mutate(number_video = n()) |>
    ungroup() |>
    filter(number_video >= 2)

  # Construct partisanship variable
  for (p in 1:nrow(ps)) {
    df <- df |>
      mutate(
        partisan = ifelse(
          party == ps$parties[p],
          case_when(
            # Strong / weak self reported identification
            !!sym(paste0("party_preference_",
                         ps$party_stub[p])) == 1 ~ 1,
            TRUE ~ 0
          ),
          partisan
        )
      )
  }

  df
}

```


# Prepare and export data 

This prepares the short-term (wave_9) and long-term (wave_10) datasets a single time.

```{r}
#| label: dataprep
prepare_data(df_09v)  |> write_rds("data/wave_9.rds")
prepare_data(df_10v) |> write_rds("data/wave_10.rds")
```


# Retain video descriptions

400 videos 

```{r}
#| label: data-retain-video-descriptions

labels_df <- 
  bind_rows(
    df_09v |> select(tiktok_id, video_desc) |> distinct(),
    df_10v |> select(tiktok_id, video_desc) |> distinct()) |>
  distinct()

labels_df |> head() |> kable()

labels_df |> write_rds("data/labels_df.rds")
```

```{r}
# Scale 0 - 1
clean_ptv <- function(x, demean = FALSE) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  x <- ifelse(x == 99, NA_real_, x)
  if (demean) x <- x - mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  x / 10
}
```


```{r}
# helper: make 1/0/NA bins from party codes
code_party_bin <- function(x, codes) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  ifelse(is.na(x), NA_integer_, as.integer(x %in% codes))
}


df_w10 <- df_w10 %>%
  dplyr::filter(SwitchedToRandom == 1) |>
  filter(!VideoDuration %in% c(-9, NA)) |>
  mutate(
    # optional readable label
    workshop = treated,
    # attrition indicator
    respondent_type = if_else(`_merge` == 3, 2L, 1L),
    respondent_type = factor(
      respondent_type,
      levels = c(1L, 2L),
      labels = c("only wave 9", "wave 9 and wave 10")
    ),
    # binary indicator: 1 if wave 9 & wave 10, else 0
    w9_w10 = if_else(`_merge` == 3, 1L, 0L),
    # raw cleaned 0–10 PTVs (99 -> NA) PRE VARS ARE DEMEANED
    ptv_w9_pre_cdu    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr1, demean = TRUE),
    # note not CDU/CSU
    ptv_w9_pre_spd    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr3, demean = TRUE),
    ptv_w9_pre_linke  = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr4, demean = TRUE),
    ptv_w9_pre_gruene = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr5, demean = TRUE),
    ptv_w9_pre_fdp    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr6, demean = TRUE),
    ptv_w9_pre_afd    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr7, demean = TRUE),
    ptv_w9_pre_bsw    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr8, demean = TRUE),
    
    # post-treatment PTVs (wave 9)
    ptv_w9_post_cdu    = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr1),
    # note not  CDU/CSU
    ptv_w9_post_spd    = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr3),
    ptv_w9_post_linke  = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr4),
    ptv_w9_post_gruene = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr5),
    ptv_w9_post_fdp    = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr6),
    ptv_w9_post_afd    = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr7),
    ptv_w9_post_bsw    = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr8),
    
    # long-term PTVs (wave 10)
    ptv_w10_cdu    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr1_W),
    # note not CDU/CSU
    ptv_w10_spd    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr3_W),
    ptv_w10_linke  = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr4_W),
    ptv_w10_gruene = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr5_W),
    ptv_w10_fdp    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr6_W),
    ptv_w10_afd    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr7_W),
    ptv_w10_bsw    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr8_W),
    
    # Combined party string (useful later)
    party = case_when(
      Parteienidentifikation %in% c(1, 2) ~ "CDU/CSU",
      Parteienidentifikation == 3 ~ "SPD",
      Parteienidentifikation == 4 ~ "AfD",
      Parteienidentifikation == 5 ~ "Gruene",
      Parteienidentifikation == 6 ~ "FDP",
      Parteienidentifikation == 7 ~ "Linke",
      Parteienidentifikation == 8 ~ "BSW",
      Parteienidentifikation == 9 ~ "Other",
      Parteienidentifikation == 10 ~ "None",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    
    # Binary indicators (1 if that party, 0 otherwise; NA stays NA)
    party_id_w9_cdu    = as.integer(Parteienidentifikation %in% c(1, 2)),
    party_id_w9_spd    = as.integer(Parteienidentifikation == 3),
    party_id_w9_afd    = as.integer(Parteienidentifikation == 4),
    party_id_w9_gruene = as.integer(Parteienidentifikation == 5),
    party_id_w9_fdp    = as.integer(Parteienidentifikation == 6),
    party_id_w9_linke  = as.integer(Parteienidentifikation == 7),
    party_id_w9_bsw    = as.integer(Parteienidentifikation == 8),
    party_id_w9_other  = as.integer(Parteienidentifikation == 9),
    party_id_w9_none   = as.integer(Parteienidentifikation == 10),
    
    party_w10 = case_when(
      as.numeric(Parteienidentifikation_W) %in% c(1, 2) ~ "CDU/CSU",
      as.numeric(Parteienidentifikation_W) == 3 ~ "SPD",
      as.numeric(Parteienidentifikation_W) == 4 ~ "AfD",
      as.numeric(Parteienidentifikation_W) == 5 ~ "Gruene",
      as.numeric(Parteienidentifikation_W) == 6 ~ "FDP",
      as.numeric(Parteienidentifikation_W) == 7 ~ "Linke",
      as.numeric(Parteienidentifikation_W) == 8 ~ "BSW",
      as.numeric(Parteienidentifikation_W) == 9 ~ "Other",
      as.numeric(Parteienidentifikation_W) == 10 ~ "None",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    
    # wave 10 binaries
    party_id_w10_cdu    = code_party_bin(Parteienidentifikation_W, c(1, 2)),
    party_id_w10_spd    = code_party_bin(Parteienidentifikation_W, 3),
    party_id_w10_afd    = code_party_bin(Parteienidentifikation_W, 4),
    party_id_w10_gruene = code_party_bin(Parteienidentifikation_W, 5),
    party_id_w10_fdp    = code_party_bin(Parteienidentifikation_W, 6),
    party_id_w10_linke  = code_party_bin(Parteienidentifikation_W, 7),
    party_id_w10_bsw    = code_party_bin(Parteienidentifikation_W, 8),
    party_id_w10_other  = code_party_bin(Parteienidentifikation_W, 9),
    party_id_w10_none   = code_party_bin(Parteienidentifikation_W, 10),
    # Labeled factor for Bundesland
    state = factor(
      state,
      levels = 1:17,
      labels = c(
        "Baden-Württemberg",
        "Bayern",
        "Berlin",
        "Brandenburg",
        "Bremen",
        "Hamburg",
        "Hessen",
        "Mecklenburg-Vorpommern",
        "Niedersachsen",
        "Nordrhein-Westfalen",
        "Rheinland-Pfalz",
        "Saarland",
        "Sachsen",
        "Sachsen-Anhalt",
        "Schleswig-Holstein",
        "Thüringen",
        "not identified"
      )
    ),
    # East (Berlin + the five new states are coded as East)
    east = case_when(
      state %in% c(
        "Berlin",
        "Brandenburg",
        "Mecklenburg-Vorpommern",
        "Sachsen",
        "Sachsen-Anhalt",
        "Thüringen"
      ) ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    # --- One dummy per state (0/1/NA) ---
    state_Baden_Wuerttemberg = case_when(
      state == "Baden-Württemberg" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Bayern = case_when(
      state == "Bayern" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Berlin = case_when(
      state == "Berlin" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Brandenburg = case_when(
      state == "Brandenburg" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Bremen = case_when(
      state == "Bremen" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Hamburg = case_when(
      state == "Hamburg" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Hessen = case_when(
      state == "Hessen" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Mecklenburg_Vorpommern = case_when(
      state == "Mecklenburg-Vorpommern" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Niedersachsen = case_when(
      state == "Niedersachsen" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Nordrhein_Westfalen = case_when(
      state == "Nordrhein-Westfalen" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Rheinland_Pfalz = case_when(
      state == "Rheinland-Pfalz" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Saarland = case_when(
      state == "Saarland" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Sachsen = case_when(
      state == "Sachsen" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Sachsen_Anhalt = case_when(
      state == "Sachsen-Anhalt" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Schleswig_Holstein = case_when(
      state == "Schleswig-Holstein" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Thueringen = case_when(
      state == "Thüringen" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    # make sure it's numeric first (if it came in as labelled/factor)
    urban_num = as.numeric(urban),
    
    urban_ballungsraum = case_when(urban_num == 1 ~ 1L, urban_num == 4 ~ NA_integer_, TRUE           ~ 0L),
    urban_stadtregion = case_when(urban_num == 2 ~ 1L, urban_num == 4 ~ NA_integer_, TRUE           ~ 0L),
    urban_mittelzentrum = case_when(urban_num == 3 ~ 1L, urban_num == 4 ~ NA_integer_, TRUE           ~ 0L),
    # optional: single binary "urban (high density) vs. rest"
    urban_high = case_when(
      urban_num %in% c(1, 2) ~ 1L,
      urban_num == 3         ~ 0L,
      urban_num == 4         ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE                   ~ NA_integer_
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(-urban_num) |>
  dplyr::select(-starts_with("party_poss"))


df_w10 <- df_w10 %>%
  # keep only needed variables (including derived ones from mutate above)
  dplyr::select(
    `_merge`,
    ID_W9,
    w9_w10,
    respondent_type,
    # wave 9 pre covariates
    Alter,
    Geschlecht,
    plz,
    east,
    citysize,
    starts_with("state_"),
    starts_with("urban_"),
    
    # social media
    Video_UserFacebook,
    Video_UserTikTok,
    Video_UserSnapchat,
    Video_UserPinterest,
    Video_UserX_ehem_Twitter,
    Video_UserTwitch,
    Politikinteresse,
    Most_important_issue,
    Competence,
    LR,
    
    # party id
    starts_with("party_id_"),
    starts_with("ptv_w"),
    
    # other
    Prior_performance_general_2,
    Solidaritat,
    Sozialer_Zusammenhalt,
    Migration,
    TikTok_User,
    TikTok_Potential,
    # treatment
    workshop,
    # job
    Beruflicher_Status,
    Berufliche_Stellung,
    # education
    Bildung_schulisch,
    Bildung_beruflich,
    # other
    Eigene_Kinder,
    HaushaltsgroBe,
    Migrationshintergrund_Eltern,
    Haushaltseinkommen,
    # outcomes (W9)
    like_w9,
    bookmark_w9,
    share_w9,
    report_w9,
    # secondary oucomes w9
    # political emotion outcomes if present
    w9_like_political_emotion,
    w9_book_political_emotion,
    w9_share_political_emotion,
    w9_report_political_emotion,
    # outcomes (W10)
    like_w10,
    bookmark_w10,
    share_w10,
    report_w10,
    # outcomes (W10)
    w10_like_political_emotion,
    w10_book_political_emotion,
    w10_share_political_emotion,
    w10_report_political_emotion
  ) %>%
  # TikTok splits and attrition indicator, then clean up names
  mutate(
    # TikTok_User (1=Ja, 2=Nein, 3=kenne TikTok nicht)
    tiktok_user_yes = case_when(
      TikTok_User == 1L ~ 1L,
      TikTok_User %in% c(2L, 3L) ~ 0L,
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    ),
    tiktok_user_no = case_when(
      TikTok_User == 2L ~ 1L,
      TikTok_User %in% c(1L, 3L) ~ 0L,
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    ),
    # TikTok_Potential (asked only if tik_tok_user == 2)
    # 1=Ja, 2=Nein; NA when not asked (has account or doesn’t know TikTok)
    tiktok_potential_yes = case_when(
      TikTok_User == 2L & TikTok_Potential == 1L ~ 1L,
      TikTok_User == 2L & TikTok_Potential == 2L ~ 0L,
      TikTok_User %in% c(1L, 3L)                 ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE                                       ~ NA_integer_
    ),
    tiktok_potential_no = case_when(
      TikTok_User == 2L & TikTok_Potential == 2L ~ 1L,
      TikTok_User == 2L & TikTok_Potential == 1L ~ 0L,
      TikTok_User %in% c(1L, 3L)                 ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE                                       ~ NA_integer_
    ),
    # Define outcome (1 = attrited, 0 = stayed)
    attrit = ifelse(w9_w10 == 1, 0, 1)
  ) %>%
  # drop observations without treatment status and standardize names
  filter(!is.na(workshop)) %>%
  rename_with(tolower)

df_w10 |> write_rds("data/df_w9_w10.rds")

```
